name: Waylon Jennings
title: Last known photo of Waylon Jennings, taken on July 3, 2001 as he arrived at Chet Atkins' funeral at the Ryman Auditorium in Nashville, about seven months before his death.
description: The last known photo of Waylon Jennings was taken on July 3, 2001, as he arrived at the funeral of guitarist Chet Atkins at the Ryman Auditorium in Nashville. Already in long decline from complications of diabetes, Jennings was using a wheelchair that day; he would have his left foot amputated five months later, in December 2001. He died on February 13, 2002, at his home in Chandler, Arizona, in his sleep, of complications from the disease. He was 64 years old.

lyrics.text:
I was a dam builder
Across a river deep and wide
Where steel and water did collide
A place called Boulder, on the wild Colorado
I slipped and fell into the wet concrete below
They buried me in that gray tomb that knows no sound
But I am still around
I'll always be around
And around, and around, and around
And around, and around, and around…

bio: Waylon Arnold Jennings was an American singer-songwriter and a pioneer of the outlaw country movement. He began his career as bassist for Buddy Holly, famously giving up his seat on the chartered flight that crashed on February 3, 1959, “The Day the Music Died”, killing Holly, Ritchie Valens, and J. P. “The Big Bopper” Richardson. With Willie Nelson, Johnny Cash, and Kris Kristofferson he later formed the supergroup The Highwaymen. He was inducted into the Country Music Hall of Fame in 2001, just months before his death.

location.name: Ryman Auditorium, 116 Rep. John Lewis Way N, Nashville, TN 37219
